summary: The plan follows the established GeometrySystem pattern closely. Adding a 4th mode is a well-supported extension point — the modeManager, quality system, and debug overlay all handle N modes generically. No structural changes are needed to existing interfaces or infrastructure.

interfaces_affected.name: VolumetricShape
interfaces_affected.change: Union type extended with 'crystalCluster' | 'geode'; VOLUMETRIC_SHAPES array extended. Backward-compatible addition.

architectural_impacts.detail: Adding crystalCluster and geode to VOLUMETRIC_SHAPES means all modes that use random shape selection (pointCloud, ribbonField, particleField) can now randomly pick these shapes. This is noted as intentional enrichment but is a cross-mode side effect that may subtly change the visual character of existing modes.

modeManager accepts ModeEntry[] with no hard limit. Adding a 4th entry is trivially supported. Mode rotation interval (90-180s seeded) will now cycle across 4 modes instead of 3 — slightly longer before revisiting any given mode.

Crystal uses maxPoints * 0.8 derived from the existing quality.maxPoints field. No new QualityProfile property needed. The 0.8x multiplier is applied at construction time in main.ts, consistent with how other modes derive their counts.

Two new GLSL files follow the same uniform/attribute interface as pointWarp. The noise3d.glsl prepend pattern is reused. No new uniforms or attributes are introduced, keeping the shader contract stable.
Debug modes array in main.ts is extended with a 4th entry. getModeName and getPointCount use modeManager.activeIndex as array index — adding the entry at index 3 is safe.

risks.description: New volumetric shapes leak into existing modes
risks.mitigation: The plan acknowledges this as intentional. If undesired, crystal shapes could be kept in a separate array and only used by crystalField. Monitor visual regression in existing modes.

risks.description: Uniform count mismatch in plan text
risks.mitigation: Plan states '18 uniforms' but pointWarp.vert.glsl declares 19 uniforms plus uOpacity in the fragment shader. Implementation should match the actual shader interface (all 20 uniforms), not the plan's count. Minor documentation inaccuracy, no code risk.

Step 6 includes explicit shader compile verification. The quantized-noise approach (floor(noise * 4.0) / 4.0) and step-edge fragment are straightforward GLSL. Risk is standard for any new shader but mitigated by reusing the proven noise3d.glsl and uniform interface.

dependency_check.US-026: Required — provides the GeometrySystem interface, modeManager, and quality tier system that crystal mode plugs into.
dependency_check.US-041: Required — provides the volumetricPoints generator and existing 3D point cloud infrastructure that crystal extends.

acceptance_criteria_coverage.Second mode is volumetric and point-base: Covered by steps 1-3: crystalField uses THREE.Points + BufferGeometry with 3D volumetric shapes.
acceptance_criteria_coverage.Mode uses 3D deformation: Covered by step 2: vertex shader applies quantized 3D noise displacement with bass-driven scaling.
acceptance_criteria_coverage.No 2D line or flat plane geometries rema: Covered by step 4: explicit audit for THREE.Line, THREE.LineSegments, THREE.PlaneGeometry, Canvas2D.
acceptance_criteria_coverage.Mode switching preserves audio pipeline: Covered by design: modeManager handles transitions via opacity crossfade without touching the audio graph. Test US-044-T05 verifies this.

recommendations: Consider whether crystalCluster and geode should be added to the shared VOLUMETRIC_SHAPES array or kept in a crystal-only array to avoid unintended visual changes to existing modes.
Verify the exact uniform count matches the pointWarp shader interface (19 vertex + 1 fragment = 20 total, not 18 as stated in the plan).

The plan's step 3 mentions 'same 5 attributes' but pointCloud uses position(3), color(3), size(1), aHueOffset(1), aRandom(3) — confirm all 5 are wired in crystalField to avoid shader attribute mismatch.
